title: Artificial Intelligence and Public Standards: government response to report
description: The government's response to the Committee's 2020 report Artificial Intelligence and Public Standards
summary: The government is responding to a 2020 report on Artificial Intelligence and Public Standards, outlining its plan to address the implications and recommendations of the report. This response is expected to clarify the government's stance on AI regulation, standards, and future policy developments.

title: Report and impact assessment on Copyright and Artificial Intelligence
description: A report and impact assessment on the use of copyright works in the development of artificial intelligence (AI) systems.
summary: This document is an assessment of how copyright laws apply to the use of copyrighted materials in the development of artificial intelligence systems. It examines the potential impact of AI on copyright law and vice versa.

title: Generative artificial intelligence (AI) in education
description: The Department for Education’s (DfE) position on the use of generative AI in the education sector.
summary: This document outlines the UK Department for Education's policy regarding the use of generative artificial intelligence in schools and educational settings. It provides guidance on the potential benefits and risks of generative AI in education and sets out the department's position on its use.

title: Transport artificial intelligence action plan
description: Sets out how DfT will work with the transport sector to exploit the opportunities from AI while managing potential risks.
summary: This policy document outlines the Department for Transport's plans to harness the benefits of artificial intelligence in the transport sector. The action plan aims to balance the opportunities and risks of using AI in transport, working closely with the industry.

title: Copyright and artificial intelligence: progress report
description: A progress report on the use of copyright works in the development of AI systems, under Section 137 of the Data (Use and Access) Act.
summary: This document is a progress report about the use of copyrighted material in the development of artificial intelligence systems. It appears to be part of a report mandated by a specific section of the Data (Use and Access) Act.

title: The regulation of artificial intelligence as a medical device: government response to the RHC
description: Government response to the recommendations made by the Regulatory Horizons Council (RHC) on the regulation of AI as a medical device.
summary: The government has published a policy responding to recommendations on the regulation of artificial intelligence used as medical devices, as suggested by the Regulatory Horizons Council. This policy aims to address the regulation of AI in the medical field to ensure patient safety and effectiveness.

title: ​​Ofqual’s approach to regulating the use of artificial intelligence in the qualifications sector​
description: Published in response to a request from the Secretary of State for Education and the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology.
summary: This document outlines Ofqual's policy approach to regulating the use of artificial intelligence in the qualifications sector. It aims to ensure the development and use of AI in assessments does not compromise the quality and integrity of qualifications.

title: Government response to the House of Lords Select Committee on Artificial Intelligence
description: Government response to the Lords Select Committee on Artificial Intelligence report ‘AI in the UK: No Room for Complacency’.
summary: This document outlines the government's response to the House of Lords' report on artificial intelligence, specifically their plans and strategies to address artificial intelligence concerns in the UK. It likely discusses the government's approach to ensuring the UK benefits from AI advancements while mitigating potential risks and challenges.

title: AI and Public Standards – Terms of Reference
description: Terms of Reference and FAQs for the Committee's review into artificial intelligence and public standards.
summary: The committee is reviewing the use of artificial intelligence in public settings to establish standards. This review aims to consider the implications and potential guidelines for AI applications in the public sphere.

title: CSPL submission to Science and Technology Committee inquiry on Governance of AI
description: CSPL has submitted written evidence to the Science and Technology Committee's inquiry on Governance of Artificial Intelligence.
summary: The Committee on Standards in Public Life (CSPL) has submitted a written response to a parliamentary inquiry about the role of government in regulating artificial intelligence. Their submission aims to provide guidance on governance of AI as part of the inquiry.
